title: Outcomes-tracking process (organizer self-report, no PII, published waste-estimation method) + re-review cadence
context: repair-cafe-kits collects no telemetry and no attendee PII, so impact is measured by organizer self-report. The self-report template must be privacy-preserving: events run, items assessed/repaired by category, and an estimated waste-diverted figure — never attendee names or contact details. The waste-diverted estimate must use a published, cited per-category weight method, clearly labeled as an estimate (not measured tonnage), with confirmed counts kept separate from estimates. A re-review cadence must be defined (at least annually) to keep content current, with electrical units re-validated by the SME and stale units flagged via lastReviewed.
objective: Design and document the organizer self-report template (no PII), publish the per-category waste-estimation methodology with cited weight factors, and define the content re-review cadence (annual minimum, electrical units via SME, stale-content flagging via lastReviewed).
acceptanceCriteria: The organizer self-report template collects: events run, items assessed by category (count), items repaired or repair-attempted by category (count), and estimated waste diverted — no attendee names, contact details, or other personal data; a visible 'do not record personal data' note is on the template

The waste-diverted estimate methodology is published in the repo: per-category average weights are cited to authoritative, publicly-available sources; the estimate is always labeled as an estimate with its methodology, never as a measured tonnage; confirmed counts and estimates are presented separately

A re-review cadence is defined and committed: at minimum annual re-review of all content units; electrical triage units re-validated by the confirmed SME; any material change to official safety or standards guidance triggers an immediate re-review; units flagged via lastReviewed when stale
The print bundle version and date are used to identify stale paper copies in the field; a process for communicating updates to known users (e.g. a versioned changelog) is documented
The outcomes-tracking process and re-review cadence are committed as a document in the repo
output: A committed outcomes-tracking document including the PII-free organizer self-report template, published waste-estimation methodology with cited weight factors, and the defined re-review cadence.
